zh.lifeSummary: Hannah Ann Beaumont Barbee是William Barbee和Elizabeth Beaumont的女兒，於1638年4月12日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州米德萊可。她於大約1661年與Christopher P Rhodes在美國維吉尼亞州米德萊可克里斯蒂 邱奇結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1717年5月7日在她的家鄉享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hannah Ann Beaumont Barbee是William Barbee和Elizabeth Beaumont的女儿，于1638年4月12日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州米德萊可。她于大约1661年与Christopher P Rhodes在美国弗吉尼亚州米德萊可克里斯蒂 邱奇结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1717年5月7日在她的家乡享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barbee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Barby  .
English (of French Huguenot origin): Anglicized form of French Barbé (see Barbe ).
Americanized form of French Barbier and possibly also of Barbé (compare 2 above).

variants.name: Barby
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Merwin, Otto, Wolfgang.  French Celestine, Pierre.
German: habitational name from a place so named in Saxony-Anhalt.
French (mainly Dordogne): derivative of   Barbe   1.

nameMeanings.name: Hannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: ‘Blessed, prosperous’; name of Muḥammad's nurse.

en.lifeSummary: Hannah Ann Beaumont Barbee was born on 12 April 1638, in Middlesex, Virginia, British Colonial America as the daughter of William Barbee and Elizabeth Beaumont. She married Christopher P Rhodes about 1661, in Christ Church, Middlesex, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 7 May 1717, in her hometown, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Hannah Ann Beaumont Barbee est née le 12 avril 1638, à Middlesex, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, fille de William Barbee et Elizabeth Beaumont. Elle a épousé Christopher P Rhodes environ 1661, à Christ Church, Middlesex, Virginie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 7 mai 1717, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 79 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Hannah Ann Beaumont Barbeeは1638年4月12日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ミッドレセックスでWilliam BarbeeとElizabeth Beaumontの娘として生まれた。約1661年にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州ミッドレセックスチリスト チャーチでChristopher P Rhodesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1717年5月7日に故郷で79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hannah Ann Beaumont Barbee(은)는 1638년 4월 12일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 미들레세스에서 William Barbee(와)과 Elizabeth Beaumont의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1661년 미국 버지니아 주 미들레세스 크리스트 c후르치에서 Christopher P Rhodes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1717년 5월 7일 그녀의 고향에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hannah Ann Beaumont Barbee nasceu em 12 de abril de 1638, em Middlesex, Virginia, British Colonial America filha de William Barbee e Elizabeth Beaumont. Ela casou-se com Christopher P Rhodes aproximadamente 1661, em Christ Church, Middlesex, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 7 de maio de 1717, em sua cidade natal, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Hannah Ann Beaumont Barbee родилась 12 апреля 1638 г. в местности миддлесекс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. Её родителей звали William Barbee и Elizabeth Beaumont. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Christopher P Rhodes около 1661 г. в местности краист чурч, миддлесекс, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 7 мая 1717 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 79 лет.
